_note: Curated trends. Entries with a `live` block get their figures refreshed from a real API by `npm run refresh` (written to trends.cache.json); the values here are the verified fallback if a fetch fails. Entries without a `live` block are always static. The `summary` prose is curated tone the LLM lightly rewrites — it never invents figures.

trends.title: Extreme poverty keeps falling
trends.metricLabel: share of the world in extreme poverty, down from 38% in 1990
trends.summary: A generation ago, nearly 4 in 10 people lived in extreme poverty. Today it's under 1 in 10 — the steepest drop in human history, even as the global population grew by billions.

source.name: Our World in Data — World Bank (PIP)

live.label: share of the world in extreme poverty

trends.title: Fewer children are dying young
trends.metricLabel: deaths per 1,000 children under 5, down from 93 in 1990
trends.summary: The share of children who die before their fifth birthday has more than halved since 1990. That's roughly 15,000 fewer children dying every single day.

source.name: UN IGME / Our World in Data

live.label: children who die before their fifth birthday

trends.title: Renewables power a record share of the grid
trends.metricLabel: of the world's electricity from renewables, up from a small fraction in 2000
trends.summary: Renewables now generate a record share of the world's electricity — close to a third — up from a small slice two decades ago, as solar and wind scale faster than any energy source in history.

source.name: Our World in Data — Ember / Energy Institute

live.label: of the world's electricity from renewables

trends.title: Guinea worm disease is nearly gone
trends.metricLabel: human cases in 2023, down from 3.5 million in 1986
trends.summary: Guinea worm disease once infected an estimated 3.5 million people a year. Thanks to a decades-long eradication campaign, there were just 14 reported human cases in 2023 — it's on track to be the second human disease ever eradicated, after smallpox.

trends.title: The ozone layer is healing
trends.metricLabel: projected year the ozone layer fully recovers
trends.summary: After the world phased out ozone-destroying chemicals under the Montreal Protocol, the ozone layer is on track to recover to 1980 levels by around 2066 over Antarctica. It's the clearest example of a global environmental problem being fixed by coordinated action.

headline.text: "Measles cases surge to highest level in years"
headline.note: The kind of alarming, true-but-narrow headline that dominates a news feed.

trend.text: Measles deaths have still fallen by roughly 80% since 2000, as global vaccination coverage climbed and the vaccine prevented an estimated 60 million deaths.
